title: HMRC PAYE in Numbers — Monthly Payroll Statistics
short_description: Monthly UK payrolled employees and median pay by region, sector, age, and pay band from HMRC Real Time Information.
long_description: Official joint HMRC-ONS monthly statistics on payrolled employees and earnings derived from Pay As You Earn Real Time Information submissions. Covers 30+ million employees with breakdowns by NUTS1/2/3 region, Standard Industrial Classification sector, age band, and pay band. Provides flash estimates within 6 weeks of month-end with ~85% record coverage, followed by final estimates. An essential leading labour market indicator for macroeconomic modelling and sector-level employment analysis.
provider: HM Revenue and Customs / Office for National Statistics
geographic_coverage: UK national; NUTS1/2/3 regions; local authorities
access_method: Free bulk download; ONS API
schema_fields: Payrolled_Employees|integer|Total payrolled employee count by breakdown;Median_Pay|currency|Median earnings in GBP;Mean_Pay|currency|Mean earnings in GBP;Region|string|NUTS region classification;Industry_SIC|string|Standard Industrial Classification code;Pay_Band|string|Pay band bracket;Age_Group|string|Employee age cohort
tags: payroll,employment,earnings,regional,sector,rti,labour market,wages,paye,real time information
notes: Flash estimates published ~6 weeks after month end; final estimates follow. Seasonally adjusted and unadjusted series available. Joint HMRC/ONS publication.

title: National Grid ESO — Carbon Intensity API
short_description: Real-time and forecast carbon intensity (gCO2/kWh) for GB electricity system with 30-minute granularity and 96-hour forecast.
long_description: The National Energy System Operator (NESO) provides a free public REST API delivering actual and forecast carbon intensity for the Great Britain electricity grid, updated every 30 minutes. Covers 96+ hour forecast horizon using machine-learning nowcasting. Regional breakdown across 14 GB zones also available. Essential for energy-focused financial firms modelling dispatch economics, renewable asset valuations, and carbon-weighted power purchase agreements.
access_method: Free API — no registration required
schema_fields: from|timestamp|Period start ISO8601 UTC;to|timestamp|Period end ISO8601 UTC;intensity.forecast|decimal|Forecast carbon intensity gCO2/kWh;intensity.actual|decimal|Actual carbon intensity gCO2/kWh;intensity.index|string|Qualitative band (very low/low/moderate/high/very high)
tags: carbon intensity,electricity,grid,real time,forecast,emissions,energy,renewable,carbon,dispatch economics
notes: No authentication required. Historical archive available. 14 regional endpoints. Also see Elexon BMRS for generation mix and balancing data.

title: Elexon BMRS — Balancing Mechanism Reporting System
short_description: Real-time UK electricity generation mix, demand, system frequency, and BM unit-level data from National Grid Elexon.
long_description: Elexon's public Balancing Mechanism Reporting Service API provides live and historical electricity system data for Great Britain including generation by fuel type (wind, solar, gas, nuclear, coal), system demand forecasts, and BM unit-level bid/offer data. Data updated in 5–60-minute intervals by type. Royalty-free perpetual licence with attribution. Used by energy traders, power desks, and sustainability analysts for intraday market operations and generation-mix analysis.
licence: Elexon Limited Perpetual Licence (royalty-free, non-exclusive) — https://bmrs.elexon.co.uk/
access_method: Free API — no key required for public endpoints
schema_fields: PublishingPeriodStartTime|timestamp|Period start UTC;PublishingPeriodEndTime|timestamp|Period end UTC;Generation_MW|decimal|Generated capacity in MW;Fuel_Type|string|GAS/WIND/NUCLEAR/COAL/HYDRO etc;Demand_MW|decimal|System demand in MW;System_Frequency_Hz|decimal|Grid frequency in Hertz
tags: balancing mechanism,electricity,generation,demand,grid,real time,fuel mix,settlement,dispatch,energy
notes: Attribution required: 'Contains BMRS data © Elexon Limited'. Developer portal at developer.data.elexon.co.uk. Data Push Service also available.

title: HMRC VAT Registrations and Deregistrations by Industry
short_description: Quarterly and annual VAT business registrations and deregistrations by SIC sector — a leading indicator of business formation and closures.
long_description: HMRC administrative VAT Data Mart statistics covering quarterly and annual new business registrations and deregistrations by Standard Industrial Classification sector. Covers 2.33 million VAT-registered traders with regional and industry breakdowns. In 2023-24, 238,176 new registrations and 273,768 deregistrations were recorded. Acts as a high-frequency business demography signal reflecting entrepreneurship, sector health, and economic activity cycles — widely used by credit analysts and macro funds.
access_method: Free download via Nomis; ONS API
schema_fields: VAT_Registrations|integer|New registrations in period;VAT_Deregistrations|integer|Deregistrations in period;Industry_SIC|string|Standard Industrial Classification;Industry_Name|string|Sector description;Region|string|Geographic region;Reference_Period|string|Quarter or year
tags: vat,business demography,registrations,deregistrations,sic,entrepreneurship,uk business,sector,quarterly,nomis
notes: 2023-24: net -35,592 businesses. Available via Nomis API and direct GOV.UK download. Leading indicator for credit risk and sector concentration models.

title: DWP Stat-Xplore — Universal Credit and Benefit Claimants
short_description: Monthly Universal Credit, JSA, and legacy benefit claimant counts by local authority, age group, and sector via REST API.
long_description: Department for Work and Pensions' Stat-Xplore platform provides detailed Universal Credit and legacy benefit claimant counts disaggregated by local authority, ward, age cohort, and economic inactivity reason. Millions of claimants tracked through the UC migration (completing March 2026). Offers both interactive web tool and JSON REST API for programmatic access. Critical for financial sector analysis of regional labour market slack, consumer credit stress, and social support trend analysis.
access_method: Free API — registration required; web portal
schema_fields: Period|string|Month and year snapshot;Geography|string|Local authority or region code;Age_Group|string|Claimant age band;Claimant_Count|integer|Number of claimants;Benefit_Type|string|UC/JSA/legacy benefit type
tags: universal credit,benefits,claimants,dwp,welfare,geography,age,social security,labour market,api
notes: REST API with schema and table endpoints. API key required (free registration). Contact stat.xplore@dwp.gov.uk. UC migration completing March 2026.

title: ONS Weekly Shipping Indicators
short_description: Weekly vessel visit counts to 15+ major UK ports from AIS tracking — a real-time proxy for goods trade and logistics activity.
long_description: ONS publishes weekly vessel movement counts for 15+ major UK ports (Southampton, Felixstowe, Dover, Hull, Liverpool, Grimsby, Tyne, Forth, Belfast, and others) based on Automatic Identification System tracking data. Broken down by vessel type (cargo, tanker, all). Published weekly on Thursdays with 1-week lag. Provides a high-frequency leading indicator for goods trade flows used by macro analysts and port-exposed credit investors.
access_method: Free bulk download; ONS API
schema_fields: Week_Commencing|date|Monday start of week;Port_Name|string|UK port name;All_Vessels|integer|Total ship visits;Cargo_Vessels|integer|Cargo vessel visits;Tanker_Vessels|integer|Tanker visits;Unique_Ships|integer|Distinct vessel count
tags: shipping,ports,trade,logistics,faster indicators,weekly,economic activity,ais,goods trade,transportation
notes: Part of ONS Faster Indicators suite. Based on AIS tracking. Contact realtime.indicators@ons.gov.uk. Published Thursdays.

title: ONS UK Spending on Credit and Debit Cards
short_description: Weekly seasonally adjusted UK consumer card spending from Bank of England CHAPS and Barclaycard POS data.
long_description: ONS experimental statistics tracking UK consumer spending volumes via aggregated debit and credit card transactions, sourced from Bank of England CHAPS settlement data and Barclaycard point-of-sale terminals. Available weekly with a 1-week lag. Provides near-real-time consumer demand signal broken down by discretionary and essential spending categories. Essential for retail sector analysis, consumer credit stress monitoring, and GDP nowcasting.
provider: Office for National Statistics / Bank of England / Barclaycard
record_count: National aggregate time series from June 2023
access_method: Free bulk download; ONS API
schema_fields: Date|date|Week ending date;Card_Spending_Index|decimal|Indexed spending value;YoY_Growth|decimal|Year-on-year percentage change;Category|string|Discretionary or essential;Seasonally_Adjusted|boolean|SA flag
tags: consumer spending,credit cards,debit cards,real time indicator,economic activity,retail,weekly,nowcasting,barclaycard,payment data
notes: Experimental official statistics. Published Fridays. Contact realtime.indicators@ons.gov.uk. CHAPS + Barclaycard POS sources.

title: AISstream.io — Global Real-Time Vessel Tracking
short_description: Free WebSocket stream of 300 msgs/sec real-time global vessel positions, speed, and identity from the AIS network.
long_description: AISstream.io provides free WebSocket API access to a global stream of Automatic Identification System data delivering vessel positions, identities, and maritime activity signals at ~300 messages per second. Financial firms use vessel tracking as a proxy for commodity trade volume, shipping congestion, and supply chain stress. Filterable by vessel MMSI and message type. Useful for monitoring port activity at Felixstowe, Southampton, and major commodity terminals as an economic activity leading indicator.
licence: Free (community licence — check publisher terms for commercial use)
access_method: Free WebSocket API — registration required within 3 seconds of connection
schema_fields: mmsi|string|Maritime Mobile Service Identity;latitude|decimal|WGS-84 latitude;longitude|decimal|WGS-84 longitude;cog|decimal|Course over ground in degrees;sog|decimal|Speed over ground in knots;nav_status|string|Navigation status code;vessel_type|string|Ship type classification
tags: ais,vessel tracking,maritime,trade,shipping,real time,websocket,global,cargo,economic signals
notes: Free tier available. Paid tiers for higher throughput. Terrestrial coverage only (not satellite). For historical AIS see MarineTraffic or Kpler (commercial).

title: OpenSky Network — Global ADS-B Aircraft Tracking
short_description: Free REST API with real-time and historical ADS-B aircraft position, altitude, and velocity — cargo movements as economic activity signal.
long_description: OpenSky Network aggregates ADS-B Mode S surveillance data from thousands of ground receivers worldwide. Free API provides aircraft state vectors including position, altitude, velocity, and callsign with 4,000 calls/day for authenticated users. Financial firms track air cargo flight activity as a proxy for supply chain velocity, business confidence, and cross-border trade flows. Historical archive covers multiple years enabling trend analysis by airline, route, or airport.
licence: Non-commercial research use — check publisher terms for commercial
access_method: Free REST API — registration required; 4,000 calls/day authenticated
schema_fields: icao24|string|ICAO24 address in hex;callsign|string|Aircraft callsign;latitude|decimal|WGS-84 latitude degrees;longitude|decimal|WGS-84 longitude degrees;geo_altitude|decimal|Geometric altitude in metres;velocity|decimal|Ground speed in m/s;true_track|decimal|Heading in degrees;vertical_rate|decimal|Climb/descent rate m/s;on_ground|boolean|Surface position flag
tags: ads-b,aircraft tracking,cargo,aviation,freight,real time,historical,economic signals,supply chain,trade
notes: Rate limits: 100 calls/day anonymous; 4,000 authenticated; 8,000 for sensor contributors. Non-commercial licence for direct access — negotiate commercial use. ADS-B contributors get enhanced access.

title: London Gazette — Statutory Notices Feed
short_description: Structured XML/JSON feed of UK statutory notices: insolvencies, winding-up petitions, company notices, and government procurement.
long_description: The London Gazette publishes machine-readable statutory notices in Atom XML and JSON formats covering corporate insolvencies, liquidation appointments, company dissolutions, bankruptcy orders, and government tender notices. Updated on weekdays. Historical notices from 1998+ available. Hedge funds and credit analysts use insolvency notice flows as early signals of credit stress and potential M&A activity. Each notice is structured with company name, registration number, notice type, and date.
access_method: Free API — REST/Atom XML; no registration required
schema_fields: notice_type|string|Notice category code;company_name|string|Entity name;company_registration|string|Companies House number;insolvency_type|string|Liquidation/administration/IVA;notice_date|date|Publication date;creditor_value|currency|Debt amount if applicable;notice_text|string|Full notice content
tags: insolvency,statutory notices,credit signals,liquidation,winding up,company notices,legal,gazette,procurement,early warning
notes: API documentation on GitHub at github.com/TheGazette/DevDocs. Atom XML and JSON-LD formats. Key use: credit risk teams monitoring insolvency flow for systemic stress. Also London Gazette XML data published as part of national digital archive.

title: VOA Non-Domestic Rating List
short_description: Complete census of 1.9M+ UK commercial properties with rateable values, property types, and addresses — updated bi-monthly.
long_description: The Valuation Office Agency publishes the full non-domestic rating list of all commercial properties in England and Wales subject to business rates. Over 1.9 million entries covering retail, office, industrial, leisure, and other commercial hereditaments with rateable values, property descriptions, and addresses. Bi-monthly epoch releases plus weekly change delta files. Asset managers use rateable value changes as signals of commercial real estate sentiment and retail sector health. UARN provides stable cross-list identifier.
licence: Crown Copyright — commercial use permitted under licence
access_method: Free bulk download via Azure Blob Storage
schema_fields: uarn|string|Unique Address Reference Number;rateable_value|currency|Rateable value in GBP;property_address|string|Full address;property_type|string|Retail/office/industrial/leisure use class;assessment_date|date|RV assessment date;hereditament_ref|string|Property identifier across lists
tags: commercial property,rateable value,business rates,uk retail,office market,industrial,voa,economic indicator,commercial real estate,hereditament
notes: Files split by local authority. UARN is stable identifier across rating list cycles. Record types 01–07; type 01 contains final RV. Weekly change files support near-real-time tracking. Commercial licence required for redistribution.

title: HM Land Registry INSPIRE Index Polygons
short_description: Monthly GML boundary polygons for all registered freehold properties in England and Wales linked to title register via INSPIRE ID.
long_description: HM Land Registry publishes monthly snapshots of INSPIRE Index Polygons providing geometric boundaries for all registered freehold properties in England and Wales in GML (Geography Markup Language) format. Each polygon links via Land Registry-INSPIRE ID to title data enabling ownership mapping. Updated first Sunday each month. Used by real estate portfolio managers, lenders, and developers to map property ownership, assess land utilisation, and monitor conveyancing transaction flows.
access_method: Free bulk download; WFS API
schema_fields: poly_id|string|Unique polygon identifier;inspire_id|string|Land Registry-INSPIRE ID;geometry|polygon|WGS-84 coordinate boundary;create_date|date|Polygon creation date;last_updated|date|Last modification timestamp
tags: land registry,property boundaries,gml,inspire,freehold,conveyancing,real estate,spatial,title register,gis
notes: GIS software required for GML processing. Polygons indicate extent, not surveyed precision. Pair with title register for ownership resolution. Updated first Sunday each month.

title: Find a Tender Service (FTS) — UK Government Procurement
short_description: Real-time OCDS-format notices and awards for all UK government contracts above £138k since January 2021.
long_description: Find a Tender Service replaced the EU OJEU/TED platform post-Brexit. Publishes procurement notices and contract awards for all UK contracting authorities subject to the Procurement Act 2023. Covers 70,000+ annual notices above threshold (typically £138k) across public and utilities sectors in OCDS JSON format. REST API provides real-time access. Critical for government capex cycle analysis, sector revenue forecasting, and supplier intelligence across defence, health, infrastructure, and technology.
geographic_coverage: UK (England, Wales, Northern Ireland; Scottish above-threshold also included)
access_method: Free REST API — no registration required
schema_fields: tender.id|string|OCDS release identifier;tender.title|string|Procurement notice title;tender.value.amount|decimal|Contract value GBP;tender.procurementMethod|string|Open/restricted/limited;buyer.name|string|Contracting authority;awards.date|date|Contract award date;items.classification.scheme|string|CPV code
tags: procurement,contracts,tenders,government spending,capex,public sector,ocds,supplier intelligence,cabinet office,find a tender
notes: Launched 24 February 2025 under Procurement Act 2023. API spec at find-tender.service.gov.uk/Developer/Documentation. OCDS format. Replaces EU TED for UK procurement.

title: UKRI Gateway to Research
short_description: 170,000+ UK publicly-funded research projects with grant amounts, institutions, outcomes, and funder breakdowns since 2006.
long_description: UKRI's Gateway to Research (GtR) database covers 170,000+ publicly funded research projects from all research councils (AHRC, BBSRC, EPSRC, ESRC, MRC, NERC, STFC, Innovate UK). Includes project references, grant amounts, institutions, researchers, collaborators, and outcomes. Updated quarterly. Used for government R&D expenditure analysis, institutional research investment tracking, innovation-linked sector analysis, and early-stage technology trend identification by venture and growth investors.
access_method: Free REST API — no registration required
schema_fields: project.id|string|Project reference code;project.title|string|Research project title;project.fundingAmount|decimal|GBP funding value;project.status|string|Active/Closed/Withdrawn;project.startDate|date|Project start;organisation.name|string|Institution name;organisation.organisationType|string|HEI/SME/public body;fundingBody|string|EPSRC/MRC/STFC etc
tags: research grants,ukri,epsrc,mrc,stfc,innovation,r&d,institutions,capex,technology trends
notes: API v2.7.5. R packages available on GitHub. Research England excluded. Student data from February 2015 only. Quarterly updates January/April/July/October.

title: HM Treasury OSCAR — Central Government Spending
short_description: UK government departmental spending outturn and budgets by programme and budget type, covering all central government departments.
long_description: OSCAR (Online System for Central Accounting and Reporting) consolidates financial data from all UK government departments for Public Expenditure Statistical Analyses. Contains departmental spending outturn and budgets by DEL (budget), AME (non-controllable), and programme structure. 2024-25 DEL total £596.4 billion. Published quarterly via PESA collections and annually as full outturn. Essential for government revenue forecasting, departmental capex cycle analysis, and sovereign risk assessment.
geographic_coverage: UK central government; devolved administrations; NDPBs; public corporations
access_method: Free bulk download via PESA collections
schema_fields: organisation_code|string|Department/body identifier;organisation_name|string|Department or NDPB name;budget_type|string|DEL/AME/Non-Budget;programme_code|string|Treasury programme reference;spending_amount|decimal|GBP outturn or budget;fiscal_year|string|2024-25 format;function|string|Functional classification
sample_rows: 2024-25|Department of Health and Social Care|DEL|180000000000;2024-25|Ministry of Defence|DEL|54000000000
tags: government spending,pesa,departmental budget,outturn,capex,fiscal policy,public expenditure,hm treasury,del,ame
notes: Published as OSCAR II from March 2025. Consolidation system — not transaction-level. PESA releases July, December, February. Annual outturn published June.

title: ONS Public Sector Employment Statistics
short_description: Quarterly UK public sector headcount by department, NHS, local government, and public corporations — time series from 2000.
long_description: ONS publishes quarterly headcount data for UK public sector employment across central government, local government, NHS, and public corporations. December 2025: 6.19 million total (record 4.06 million central government). Available with seasonal adjustment and non-adjusted series. Time series from 2000. Used for government labour cost analysis, public-private employment ratio monitoring, and wage inflation scenario modelling by macro and credit investors.
access_method: Free bulk download; ONS time series API
schema_fields: Reference_Period|string|Quarter and year;Public_Sector_Total|decimal|Headcount thousands;Central_Government|decimal|Central govt headcount;Local_Government|decimal|Local govt headcount;NHS|decimal|NHS headcount;Public_Corporations|decimal|Public corp headcount;Seasonally_Adjusted|boolean|SA flag
tags: public sector,employment,civil service,nhs,local government,headcount,quarterly,labour market,ons
notes: December 2025: +0.7% YoY. Central govt at record 4.06m. EMP02/EMP03 datasets for sectoral breakdown.

title: UK Export Finance (UKEF) — Guarantee and Transaction Data
short_description: Annual export credit guarantees, loans, and insurance policies by country and sector — £14.5B supporting 667 exporters in 2024-25.
long_description: UK Export Finance publishes annual statistics on export credit guarantees, direct loans, and insurance policies issued to UK exporters. 2024-25: £14.5 billion supporting 667 exporters (496 SMEs) across 70,000 jobs. Data covers guarantee type, destination country, sector, and exporter size. Used for export-sector capex analysis, emerging market credit risk assessment, and trade finance pipeline intelligence by credit and macro investors.
geographic_coverage: UK exporters; transactions by destination country
access_method: Free download — annual reports and appendices
schema_fields: exporter_name|string|UK business name;exporter_size|string|SME or large;sector|string|Industry classification;destination_country|string|Export destination;guarantee_type|string|ECIC/political risk/bond;transaction_value|decimal|GBP amount;transaction_date|date|Issue or draw date
tags: export finance,trade,credit guarantees,sme,ukef,emerging markets,trade finance,capex,sector intelligence,country risk
notes: Annual report published July. Transaction-level data in appendices. FORMAT: PDF — requires AI extraction for structured data.

title: Payment Systems Regulator — Payment Statistics
short_description: Quarterly UK payment volumes, values, and market share across Faster Payments, Bacs, CHAPS, and card systems.
long_description: The PSR publishes comprehensive quarterly payment statistics tracking transaction volumes and values across the UK's major payment systems. 2024: Faster Payments 5.1 billion transactions; Bacs 6.8 billion; CHAPS 53.3 million transactions valued at £93.9 trillion. APP fraud reimbursement statistics also published (99% reimbursement rate post-October 2024). Used by fintech investors, bank analysts, and macro funds tracking consumer and wholesale payment flow trends.
access_method: Free bulk download; interactive dashboard
schema_fields: Payment_System|string|Faster Payments/Bacs/CHAPS/Cards;Transaction_Volume|integer|Number of transactions;Transaction_Value|decimal|Total value in GBP;Market_Share_Volume|decimal|Percentage of total transactions;Period|string|Quarter or year
tags: payment systems,faster payments,bacs,chaps,card payments,market share,transaction volumes,fintech,psr,app fraud
notes: PSR merging into FCA (date TBC). APP reimbursement stats new from October 2024. Confirmation of Payee adoption data also published.

title: LINK ATM Network Statistics
short_description: Quarterly UK ATM cash withdrawal volumes, values, and geographic distribution across 42,000+ machines — a proxy for local economic activity.
long_description: LINK publishes quarterly and annual statistics on UK ATM cash withdrawals covering 42,403 machines. In 2025, consumers withdrew £76.7 billion across 1.27 billion transactions (average £92.11 per withdrawal). Regional breakdown shows significant geographic variation — Northern Ireland most cash-dependent at £2,249 average annual withdrawal per capita. Network contracted 5% year-on-year. Valuable as a high-frequency economic activity and consumer confidence proxy at local authority level.
access_method: Free bulk download; interactive dashboard
schema_fields: Region|string|UK region or local authority;Withdrawal_Volume|integer|Number of ATM transactions;Withdrawal_Value|decimal|Total value withdrawn GBP;Average_Withdrawal|decimal|Mean withdrawal GBP;ATM_Count|integer|Number of ATMs;Period|string|Quarter or year
tags: atm,cash withdrawals,economic activity,consumer behaviour,retail,fintech,regional,link,cash economy,local authority
notes: 2025: 4% value decline, 8% transaction decline vs 2024. Busiest day: 19 December 2025 at £355m. Fee-paying ATMs growing segment. Network contraction tracking useful for financial inclusion analysis.

title: UK Finance — Annual Fraud Statistics
short_description: Annual and half-yearly UK fraud losses by category: APP fraud, card fraud, unauthorised fraud, and bank prevention data.
long_description: UK Finance publishes comprehensive annual and half-yearly fraud statistics covering all fraud types affecting UK financial services. 2024: £1.17 billion total losses across 3.3 million incidents. H1 2025: £629.3 million losses (2.09 million cases). APP fraud H1 2025: £257.5 million (+12% YoY). Banks prevented £870 million in unauthorised fraud H1 2025 (+20% vs H1 2024). Used by fintech investors, bank equity analysts, fraud technology vendors, and regulators to track systemic fraud risk and prevention effectiveness.
licence: UNKNOWN — check publisher terms; annual reports publicly available
access_method: Free download — annual and half-year reports
schema_fields: Fraud_Type|string|APP/Card/Cheque/Authorised/Unauthorised;Fraud_Losses|decimal|Losses in GBP;Fraud_Cases|integer|Number of reported cases;Prevention_Value|decimal|Value prevented by security GBP;Period|string|Year or half-year
tags: fraud,financial crime,app fraud,card fraud,unauthorised,prevention,banks,fintech,compliance,money laundering
notes: H1 2025 published May 2025. Annual report published March each year. Negotiate raw data licence directly with UK Finance.

title: FCA Financial Lives Survey
short_description: Biennial survey of 17,950 UK adults covering savings, debt, vulnerability, product holdings, and financial resilience since 2017.
long_description: The FCA's Financial Lives Survey is a biennial study of UK adults' financial behaviour, vulnerability, and resilience. The 2024 survey (17,950 respondents, February–June 2024) shows 24% of UK adults (13.1 million) have low financial resilience; 49% (26.4 million) show vulnerability characteristics. Covers savings patterns, debt levels, housing tenure, financial products, digital exclusion. Longitudinal tracker tables enable 4-survey comparisons since 2017. Data available via the Geographic Data Service.
access_method: Free download; Geographic Data Service
schema_fields: Respondent_ID|string|Unique identifier;Financial_Resilience|string|High/medium/low;Vulnerability_Status|string|Vulnerable or not;Savings_Level|decimal|Total savings GBP;Debt_Level|decimal|Total debt GBP;Housing_Tenure|string|Owned/mortgaged/rented;Product_Holdings|string|Financial products held
sample_rows: 2024|24% low resilience (13.1m)|49% vulnerable (26.4m)|1-in-10 zero cash savings|29% mortgaged
tags: consumer finance,vulnerability,financial resilience,savings,debt,household finance,survey,fca,uk adults,financial inclusion
notes: 2024 data via Geographic Data Service by September 2025. Tracker tables for longitudinal analysis across all 4 surveys. New expanded sector-specific slide reports from 2024.

title: ONS Wealth and Assets Survey
short_description: Biennial household wealth survey covering property, pensions, financial assets, and debt by age and income — 17,541 households.
long_description: The ONS Wealth and Assets Survey is a biennial longitudinal study of household wealth composition. Wave 7 (April 2020–March 2022) covered 17,541 households. Median household total wealth £293,700: property 40%, pensions 35%, financial assets 14%, physical 10%. Covers wealth inequality, asset composition by demographic, and trends across financial crisis, pandemic, and recovery periods. Available via UK Data Service with full microdata. Essential for wealth management strategy, pension fund gap analysis, and real estate market research.
access_method: Free download; UK Data Service (microdata)
schema_fields: Household_ID|string|Unique identifier;Total_Wealth|decimal|Median household wealth GBP;Property_Wealth|decimal|Net property value GBP;Pension_Wealth|decimal|Private pension GBP;Financial_Wealth|decimal|Net financial assets GBP;Age_Group|string|HRP age band;Income_Quintile|string|Income quintile
tags: household wealth,pensions,financial assets,property,inequality,demographics,savings,debt,macroeconomics,wealth management
notes: Full microdata via UK Data Service (registration required). Wave 8 expected 2024-25. Interactive tools on ONS website for aggregate analysis.

title: UK IPO Trademarks Register Bulk Data
short_description: Complete UK trademark applications and registrations with owner, classification, and status — brand investment as a corporate activity signal.
long_description: Bulk download of the UK Intellectual Property Office trademark register containing all registered marks and pending applications since 1876. In tab-separated text format with applicant details, goods and services classifications (Nice Classification), registration dates, and status. Used by financial firms to track brand investment patterns, corporate IP portfolios, and market entry signals across sectors. Weekly Trade Marks Journal provides new applications and registrations. API access planned as part of One IPO transformation.
access_method: Free bulk download from GOV.UK
schema_fields: registration_number|string|Unique trademark registration ID;trade_mark|string|Registered trademark text or description;applicant_name|string|Company or individual name;goods_services|string|Nice Classification codes for goods/services;registration_date|date|Date registered;status|string|Registered/Pending/Expired/Cancelled
tags: trademarks,brand portfolio,ip registration,sector intelligence,market signals,competitive analysis,brand investment,corporate activity,ipo,niceclassification
notes: API access planned 2026 as part of One IPO transformation. Weekly Trade Marks Journal published. Useful for M&A due diligence and brand concentration analysis.

title: UK IPO Patents Journal — Published Patent Applications
short_description: Weekly published UK patent applications with applicant names, IPC classifications, and abstracts — R&D and innovation tracking signal.
long_description: Weekly publication of UK patent applications and granted patents from the e-Patents Journal (March 2008 onwards). Contains publication numbers, application numbers, filing dates, applicant/inventor names, titles, abstracts, and International Patent Classification codes. Used for sector-wide R&D intelligence, competitive patent landscaping, and innovation trend identification by venture capital, growth equity, and technology-focused fund managers. XML format enables programmatic analysis at scale.
licence: Crown Copyright — open data; check publisher terms for commercial use
record_count: UNKNOWN — thousands of applications per week
access_method: Free bulk download (PDF/XML); API planned 2026
schema_fields: publication_number|string|Patent publication identifier;application_number|string|Original patent application number;filing_date|date|Application filing date;applicant_names|string|Patent applicant names;inventor_names|string|Inventor names;abstract_title|string|Patent title and abstract;ipc_classification|string|IPC codes
tags: patents,r&d,innovation,technology trends,applicant intelligence,ipc classification,competitive intelligence,venture capital,sector analysis,ipo
notes: Bulk API access planned 2026 via One IPO transformation. Weekly XML journals from March 2008. Pair with EPO Espacenet for broader patent landscape. Useful for technology sector due diligence.

title: ACER REMIT — EU Wholesale Energy Transaction Data
short_description: Quarterly aggregated EU wholesale electricity and gas trading transactions via ACER CHEST — 100M+ monthly transactions from May 2025.
long_description: ACER published its first official REMIT (Regulation on Wholesale Energy Market Integrity and Transparency) transaction data in May 2025 via the CHEST application on the AEGIS platform. Covers EU wholesale electricity and gas market transactions including counterparty IDs, volumes, prices, and product classifications. ~7.2 million daily transactions; 100+ million transactions in Q1 2025 alone. Used by energy traders, power desks, macro hedge funds with energy exposure, and regulatory compliance teams monitoring wholesale energy market manipulation.
provider: ACER (Agency for the Cooperation of Energy Regulators)
licence: ACER published data — check publisher terms
access_method: Free via ACER CHEST on AEGIS platform
schema_fields: market_participant_id|string|REMIT unique identifier;counterparty_id|string|Counterparty identifier;transaction_volume|decimal|Contract volume;volume_unit|string|MWh/GWh;price_per_unit|decimal|Price;product_type|string|Electricity or gas;transaction_date|date|Date of transaction
tags: remit,energy trading,wholesale markets,gas,electricity,market transparency,transaction data,regulatory reporting,acer,price signals
notes: First REMIT data release May 2025. Nine datasets covering transactions and market participants. Quarterly updates. SFTP/API access in development. Covers EU; separate UK REMIT data via OFGEM.

title: Bank of England Agents' Summary of Business Conditions
short_description: Quarterly qualitative and scored regional economic intelligence from 12 BoE agents across 700+ UK businesses covering wages, demand, and credit.
long_description: Monthly and quarterly qualitative and quantitative intelligence collected by Bank of England's 12 regional agents from at least 700 UK businesses covering all sectors. Published with Agents' Scores on a −5 to +5 scale for employment intentions, wage settlements, consumer demand, investment intentions, and credit conditions. Data extends from 1999. Financial firms use this for regional economic forecasting, wage inflation monitoring, and early-stage sector health assessment well before official ONS data is released.
access_method: Free bulk download (Excel, PDF)
schema_fields: Period|string|Reporting quarter;Region|string|BoE regional agency;Employment_Intentions|decimal|Score -5 to +5;Wage_Settlements|decimal|Average wage growth %;Inflation_Outlook|decimal|Expected CPI trend score;Credit_Supply|decimal|Credit availability score;Consumer_Demand|decimal|Retail spending score;Sector|string|Manufacturing/services/retail/construction
sample_rows: 2025 Q2|National|Employment intentions slightly negative|Wage settlements 3.9%|Inflation expected to ease 2026
tags: regional economy,agents survey,wage inflation,employment intentions,business conditions,sector intelligence,credit supply,monetary policy,qualitative data,bank of england
notes: Agents' Scores Excel file available for download. Used alongside Decision Maker Panel surveys. 8-week interview cycles. Published concurrently with MPC meetings.

title: EPC Energy Performance Certificate Bulk Data
short_description: 30M+ energy performance certificates for UK buildings with A–G ratings, recommendations, and UPRN linkage since 1995.
long_description: Comprehensive register of Energy Performance Certificates for England, Wales, and Scotland covering nearly 30 million buildings since 1995. Contains energy efficiency ratings (A–G), potential efficiency ratings, lodgement dates, UPRN-linked addresses, and assessor recommendations for improvements. Updated continuously. Financial firms use for climate transition risk analysis (brown discount/green premium), real estate portfolio decarbonisation planning, mortgage book risk assessment, and physical risk scoring under TCFD.
provider: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities / MHCLG
licence: Restrictive — Data Protection Act 2018 / GDPR compliance required; Royal Mail IP restrictions apply
access_method: API and bulk download — registration and terms acceptance required
schema_fields: lmk_key|string|Unique lodgement identifier;uprn|string|Unique Property Reference Number;current_energy_rating|string|A–G band;potential_energy_rating|string|Potential efficiency rating;lodgement_date|date|Date lodged on register;inspection_date|date|Inspection date;posttown|string|Postal town;recommendation_codes|string|Efficiency improvement codes
tags: epc,energy efficiency,building performance,property ratings,climate risk,asset valuation,tcfd,brown discount,green premium,mortgage risk
notes: Requires account registration and DPA 2018/GDPR terms acceptance. CSVW JSON schema available. 30M+ records covering residential and commercial stock. Key input for climate stress testing under BoE/PRA scenarios.

title: BIS OTC Derivatives Statistics
short_description: Semi-annual global OTC derivatives outstanding notional and gross market values by asset class across 12 major dealer jurisdictions.
long_description: BIS semi-annual statistics on outstanding notional amounts and gross market values of over-the-counter derivatives contracts. Covers interest rate, foreign exchange, credit default swaps, equity, and commodity derivatives. Compiled from 12 major reporting countries covering 65 large dealers. ~2,500 time series from 1998. Essential for fixed income and FX desks monitoring derivative market concentration and systemic risk, and for macro hedge funds assessing cross-asset leverage cycles.
geographic_coverage: 12 major reporting jurisdictions including UK, US, France, Germany, Japan, Switzerland
access_method: Free BIS SDMX API; free bulk download
schema_fields: Reporting_Country|string|Country of dealer residence;Asset_Class|string|Interest rate/FX/credit/equity/commodity;Notional_Amount|decimal|Notional value outstanding USD billions;Gross_Market_Value|decimal|Current replacement cost USD billions;Counterparty_Sector|string|Reporting dealer/other fin institution/non-financial
sample_rows: 2024 H1|Interest rate|USD 503 trillion notional|USD 6.5 trillion gross market value;2024 H1|FX|USD 131 trillion notional|USD 2.4 trillion gross market value
tags: otc derivatives,interest rate swaps,cds,fx forwards,notional value,gross market value,systemic risk,bis,global markets,derivatives
notes: Regular semi-annual release covers 12 countries; triennial survey covers broader population. BIS SDMX API at data.bis.org. Critical for cross-asset leverage cycle analysis.

title: BIS Locational and Consolidated Banking Statistics
short_description: Quarterly cross-border bank claims and liabilities by reporting country, counterparty country, currency, and sector — 43 reporting countries.
long_description: BIS Locational and Consolidated Banking Statistics track quarterly international claims and liabilities of banks resident in 43 reporting countries on counterparties in 200+ jurisdictions. Locational statistics report on-balance-sheet positions by instrument, currency, sector, and counterparty nationality; consolidated statistics cover worldwide positions net of inter-office accounts. Over 8,000 time series from 1983. Essential for global asset allocators assessing cross-border credit exposure, currency denomination risks, and sector-level international lending patterns.
access_method: Free BIS SDMX API; bulk download; Snowflake data marketplace
schema_fields: Reporting_Country|string|Country of reporting bank;Counterparty_Country|string|Country of counterparty;Currency|string|USD/EUR/JPY/GBP/CHF/local;Sector|string|Bank/non-bank financial/corporate/government;Claims_Amount|decimal|Outstanding claims USD billions;Liabilities_Amount|decimal|Outstanding liabilities USD billions
tags: cross-border banking,bank claims,liabilities,international exposure,currency risk,sector breakdown,consolidated banking,bis,fixed income,sovereign risk
notes: Locational basis: residence; consolidated: worldwide positions. Non-bank sector includes hedge funds and money market funds. Also available on Snowflake Data Marketplace.

title: AFME European Securitisation Data
short_description: Quarterly European securitisation issuance and outstanding by asset class: CLO, RMBS, CMBS, ABS, and SME ABS across 8+ geographies.
long_description: AFME publishes quarterly securitisation data covering European issuance and outstanding volumes segmented by CLOs, RMBS, CMBS, ABS, and SME ABs. Includes geographic breakdown (UK, Germany, France, Spain, Italy, Nordic, Pan-European) and placement statistics. Q2 2025 total European issuance: EUR 78.9 billion (49.5% placed). Pan-European CLOs led 2025 at EUR 60.5 billion YTD. Critical for macro hedge funds, structured credit desks, and fixed income allocators tracking European securitisation supply dynamics and collateral quality.
provider: Association for Financial Markets in Europe (AFME)
licence: AFME proprietary — public summary reports available free; raw data requires licence
record_count: 600+ time series (6 asset classes × 8+ geographies × 2+ metrics)
geographic_coverage: Pan-European; UK; Germany; France; Spain; Italy; Nordic
access_method: Free download of quarterly PDF reports; contact AFME for raw data licence
schema_fields: Asset_Class|string|CLO/RMBS/CMBS/ABS/SME-ABS/WBS;Geography|string|Pan-European/UK/Germany etc;Quarter|string|YYYY-Qn format;Issuance_Volume_EUR_bn|decimal|New issuance EUR billions;Outstanding_Volume_EUR_bn|decimal|Total outstanding EUR billions;Placed_Volume_EUR_bn|decimal|Placed amount EUR billions
tags: securitisation,clo,rmbs,cmbs,abs,european credit,structured finance,issuance volume,afme,credit desk
notes: Annual summaries and quarterly reports free on AFME website. Negotiate commercial data licence for raw Excel tables. Q3 2025 report available at afme.eu. Key for CLO managers and structured credit hedge funds.

title: IMF World Economic Outlook Database
short_description: Twice-yearly GDP, inflation, current account, and debt forecasts for 196 countries from 1980 with 2-year forward projections.
long_description: The IMF World Economic Outlook database is published twice annually (April and October) containing macroeconomic forecasts and historical data for 196 countries and regional aggregates. Covers real and nominal GDP, inflation, unemployment, government debt, current account balances, and commodity prices from 1980 with 2-year projections. ~50,000 time series. Essential for global macro hedge funds, FX desks, sovereign credit analysts, and global asset allocators monitoring growth, inflation, and fiscal risk regimes across all major economies.
licence: IMF Terms of Use — public, attribution required; https://www.imf.org/external/terms.htm
geographic_coverage: 196 countries; regional aggregates (Advanced Economies, Emerging Markets, Sub-Saharan Africa etc.)
access_method: Free direct CSV download; IMF Data Portal API; Python weo-reader package
schema_fields: Country_Code|string|ISO 3-letter code;Indicator_Code|string|NGDPD/PCPIPCH/BCA/GGXWDG_NGDP etc;Year|integer|1980–2027;Value|decimal|Indicator value;Obs_Status|string|Estimate or forecast flag

tags: gdp,inflation,macro forecast,sovereign debt,current account,imf,global economy,fx,emerging markets,hedge funds
notes: October 2025 release published March 2026. Available in SDMX and CSV. Python package 'weo-reader' on PyPI. Critical for sovereign CDS pricing and FX portfolio construction.

title: Lloyd's of London Annual Market Results
short_description: Annual and interim Lloyd's market results: £55.5B gross written premium, combined ratio, capacity, and syndicate performance by class.
long_description: Lloyd's publishes comprehensive annual and half-year financial results including gross written premium, underwriting profits and losses, combined ratios, and capital positions. 2024 full-year: GWP £55.5 billion (+6.5% YoY), underlying combined ratio 79.1%, profit before tax £9.6 billion, solvency ratio 435%. Data segmented by class of business (marine, aviation, property, casualty, reinsurance) and individual syndicate performance. Essential for insurance-linked securities investors, catastrophe bond hedge funds, and credit analysts covering Lloyd's market participants.
licence: Lloyd's proprietary — public summary reports freely available; syndicate data restricted
format: PDF reports, web portal dashboards, Excel data tables
access_method: Free download of annual/interim reports; Crystal+ API platform (registered users) for syndicate data
schema_fields: Reporting_Period|string|Full-year or half-year;Class_Of_Business|string|Marine/Aviation/Property/Liability/Reinsurance;Syndicate_Number|integer|Syndicate ID;Gross_Written_Premium_GBP_mn|decimal|Premium in GBP millions;Combined_Ratio|decimal|Loss+expense ratio %;Underwriting_Result_GBP_mn|decimal|Underwriting profit or loss
tags: insurance,lloyd's,gross written premium,combined ratio,underwriting,reinsurance,syndicate,ils,catastrophe bonds,insurance linked securities
